question_text: Which of the following will increase the strength of an electromagnet?

options.label: B
options.text: Using a plastic core instead of an iron core

options.label: C
options.text: Increasing the number of coils of wire

correct_answer: Increasing the number of coils of wire
explanation: The strength of an electromagnet depends on three main factors: the electric current flowing through it, the number of coils of wire, and the type of core material used. More coils of wire create a stronger magnetic field because each coil contributes to the overall magnetic effect. Using an iron core (not plastic) and increasing the current also strengthen the electromagnet.
